input.prompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:
1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me create a complete working solution. I'll set up a .NET project with the MCP SDK, configure stdio transport, add an HttpClient-based tool, a summarization prompt, and ensure logging goes to stderr (not stdout, which would corrupt the JSON-RPC messages).

Let me start by checking what's in the current directory and then build the solution.

data.content: Now let me set up the project file with the MCP SDK and required packages, then create the source files:

arguments.new_str:
﻿<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="ModelContextProtocol" Version="0.2.0-preview.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="9.0.4" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Http" Version="9.0.4" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

</Project>
